title: apple-health-mcp-server — Make Claude your personal health AI.

hero.headline_html:
Make Claude your
personal health AI.
hero.deck_html:
A local-first MCP server that hands your full Apple Health export to Claude.
Your runs, your sleep, your trends —
like a trainer or coach, only for you.

claude.name: Claude
claude.body_html:
Your runs last week totaled 32.4 km, average pace 5'38"/km. HRV dropped 9 ms the day after your long run — suggest an easy run or rest today.

Sleep depth also -22% that day — recovery priority.
claude.meta: Local DuckDB · 21 tools · no cloud upload

why.title: Your full Apple Health export, given to Claude — locally.
why.deck: An MCP server for the health data you'd rather not hand to a cloud — analyzed entirely on your own laptop.
why.card1_body: Not a byte leaves your laptop. Everything stays in your local DuckDB.
why.card2_body: export.xml + ECG CSV + GPX routes — every artefact Apple Health writes out.
why.card3_body: A broad, read-oriented query surface. Workouts, heart rate, sleep, ECG, State of Mind.
why.card4_body: Claude Code / Claude Desktop / Codex CLI — same data, any entry point.

usecases.title: Ask your own data anything.
usecases.deck: Just prompt Claude. It picks the right tools on its own.
usecases.card1_q: Map yesterday's run, color-coded by heart rate.
usecases.card1_a_html: Pulled GPX points with get_workout_route() and time-aligned HR with get_heart_rate_samples() — generated an HTML map colored by HR zone.
usecases.card2_q: What should today's training look like?
usecases.card2_a_html: Checked sleep, HRV, and resting HR over the past 7 days. HRV hasn't recovered from the long run yet — recommend easy run or rest today.
usecases.card3_q: Notice anything in my long-term data?
usecases.card3_a_html: VO2Max shows a clear seasonal dip in winter, recovers in spring. Body weight tends to spike +1.2 kg after long holidays. Sleep duration is +42 min only on weekends.

export.title: Export your Health data first.
export.deck: From the iPhone Health app, export everything as a single archive and transfer it to your Mac / PC. Takes a few minutes.
export.step1: Open the Health app on iPhone
export.step4: Wait a few minutes (5-15 min for several GB)
export.step5: Send to your Mac / PC via AirDrop or cloud, then unzip the archive
export.note_html: Unzipping gives you an apple_health_export/ folder — export.xml plus the ECG CSVs and GPX routes. Hand the whole folder to the import command.

install.title: Three environments, same data.
install.deck: Claude Code / Claude Desktop / Codex CLI — a few steps each.
install.uvx_step1: 1. Install uv via the official installer
install.uvx_step2: 2. Import the unzipped Apple Health export directory
install.uvx_note: ~/apple_health_export is the folder containing export.xml, ECG CSVs, and GPX files. Re-import is idempotent — duplicates are auto-dedup'd.
install.code_note: Append @latest to re-resolve the newest version on every serve. See README "Updating" for details.
install.desktop_step1: 1. Download the .mcpb bundle from GitHub Releases
install.desktop_step2: 2. Drag & drop the .mcpb onto Claude Desktop → Settings → Extensions
install.desktop_step3: 3. Run the import command in your terminal once (point it at your export.xml folder)
install.desktop_note: Claude Desktop is not available on Linux — use Claude Code or Codex CLI instead.
install.codex_body: Register as an MCP server in ~/.codex/config.toml.

tools.title: A read-oriented, broad query surface.
tools.deck: Tools modeled on the Apple Health schema. Claude composes queries; your local DuckDB answers.
tools.t_query_records: Fetch any HKQuantity / HKCategory record by type and time range.
tools.t_get_record_statistics: Per-type sum / avg / min / max aggregations.
tools.t_list_record_types: List every record type present after import.
tools.t_list_workouts: List workouts filtered by date and type.
tools.t_get_workout_details: Full metadata and stats for a single workout.
tools.t_get_workout_route: GPX route points with timestamps (joinable to HR samples).
tools.g3_desc: Apple Watch ECG waveforms and classifications.
tools.t_list_ecg_readings: List ECG readings by date (with classification and symptoms).
tools.t_get_ecg_data: Raw waveform samples for a single ECG reading.
tools.t_get_heart_rate_samples: Heart rate sample series over a range (align to a workout for map sourcing).
tools.g5_desc: Correlated records such as blood pressure.
tools.t_list_correlations: List correlations (e.g. blood pressure) by date.
tools.t_get_correlation_details: A single correlation and its child records' real values.
tools.g7_desc: DB-wide metadata and your own attributes.
tools.t_get_import_history: Past import runs and row counts.
tools.t_list_data_sources: List data sources (Apple Watch / iPhone / connected apps).
tools.t_get_me_attributes: Your own attributes — birthdate, biological sex, blood type, handedness.
tools.t_run_custom_query: Run an arbitrary SELECT (writes are rejected at the parser level).

privacy.title: Not a byte leaves your laptop.
privacy.deck: Starting from the Apple Health export, every step happens on your own machine.
privacy.point1_body: ~/.local/share/apple-health-mcp/data.duckdb (XDG_DATA_HOME compliant). One file — easy to back up, easy to delete.
privacy.point2_body: There is no code path that ships your health data anywhere. The only network access is uvx resolving server updates.
privacy.point3_body: MIT licensed. Every line on GitHub, fully auditable.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.q1: Claude Desktop doesn't run on Linux — what should I use?
faq.a1: Use Claude Code (CLI) or Codex CLI. Both speak MCP and give you the same capabilities.
faq.q2: What is uv? Do I need to install it?
faq.a2: uv is a Python package runner / installer (official: astral.sh/uv). uvx lets you run this server with a single command, so it's the recommended path.
faq.q3: Can I use this without an Apple Watch?
faq.a3: Yes. iPhone alone gives you steps, sleep, weight, nutrition and more — plenty to work with. Only a few ECG / HR-specific tools will return empty.
faq.a4: ~/.local/share/apple-health-mcp/data.duckdb (XDG_DATA_HOME compliant). Delete the file and all data is gone.
faq.q5: What happens if I re-import the same export?
faq.a5: Idempotent. Duplicate rows are deduped by content hash, so re-running yields the same DB.
